title: Adjusting: Unearned Revenue Earned
event: On November 1, Ortiz Co. received $6,000 for six months of services and credited Unearned Revenue. Prepare the December 31 adjusting entry.

lines.label: 6,000 × 2/6
lines.why: Two months of the obligation have been worked off — the liability shrinks.
lines.trap: Debiting a liability feels backwards to students. You're REDUCING what you owe.

lines.label: Nov + Dec earned
lines.why: Nov and Dec's work is now earned: $1,000/month × 2.
lines.trap: Recognize only what's EARNED — the remaining $4,000 stays parked as a liability.

memorize.body: The mirror image of the prepaid adjustment: liability converts to revenue as work happens, exactly as prepaid converts to expense.
Two months (Nov, Dec) — not one, not six. The fraction is where the points are lost.

questions.prompt: $6,000 received Nov 1 for 6 months. Revenue earned by Dec 31 = ?

distractors.feedback: Only 2 of the 6 months are done. Recognizing it all overstates revenue by 4,000.
That's the part still UNEARNED (4 remaining months). The adjustment records the 2 months earned.
That's one month. November AND December have both passed.

build.scaffold: Months earned ÷ months promised × cash received. Debit the liability down, credit revenue up.
